[User] if integrate Zendesk,can i view the chat history from Chatty with Zendesk
[CS: Hazel] Yes, you can. By integrating Zendesk with Chatty, your conversation history will be automatically saved to Zendesk as support tickets.



Here's how it works:

• When a conversation is resolved in Chatty, a ticket is automatically created in Zendesk with the full chat transcript and customer details.
• You can then view and search all your Chatty conversations directly in Zendesk.

Please note that it only syncs when the conversation is resolved, not in real-time. Therefore, if customers want to use Zendesk as a long-term chat history storage location, they need to resolve the conversation on Chatty.

[CS: Hazel] 啊，请给我一点时间给你解释一下
[CS: Hazel] 以下是这三种指标的区别：



* AI参与率：总对话中AI参与的比例。这包括任何AI回应的对话，即使后来转交给了人类代理。

* AI解决率：指AI处理的对话中，完全由AI在无需人工代理的情况下解决的比例。这是显示人工智能如何有效处理自身问题的关键指标。

* 回答率：基于你的知识库，AI能够回答客户问题的百分比。这反映了你的知识库对客户所询问主题的覆盖程度。


简而言之：

• 参与度 = 人工智能是否加入了讨论？
• 分辨率 = AI是否在没有人工帮助的情况下解决了？
• 回答率 = 人工智能是否拥有正确的知识来回应？

Regarding the issue where the AI was not transferring conversations to a human support agent, we discovered that there is a specific FAQ in your FAQs list that was conflicting with the human handover logic. This FAQ was essentially "intercepting" the request before the handover trigger could activate.

![](https://storage.crisp.chat/users/upload-1/session/-/b/5/7/9/b579ba0c04753000/image-4_uvril6.webp)
Our recommendation: we have identified two effective ways to resolve this and ensure no customer requests are missed:

Option 1: You can move all the relevant contents from that specific FAQ into the "Outside Working Hours" section. This way, whenever an agent is not available, the AI will automatically notify the user and provide instructions on how to contact your human support team instead of getting stuck in a loop.

![](https://storage.crisp.chat/users/upload-1/session/-/b/5/7/9/b579ba0c04753000/image-6_mlv0h0.webp)
Option 2: We tried to add a supplementary question to the flow. This allows the AI to suggest "Live Chat" as an option more clearly. This method helps maintain the conversation flow and ensures the system assigns the agent correctly without any interruption.

![](https://storage.crisp.chat/users/upload-1/session/-/b/5/7/9/b579ba0c04753000/image-7_1fyn4fv.webp)
We have tested these adjustments, and the AI is now working much more accurately as you can refer to the image below. Could you please check it on your end and let us know which approach you prefer?

![](https://storage.crisp.chat/users/upload-1/session/-/b/5/7/9/b579ba0c04753000/image-8_1ktji3p.webp)

[CS: Megan] Hi again

,I’ve consulted with my internal team regarding your use case (leveraging Klaviyo for mass customer outreach and transitioning to Chatty for handling replies). 

Here is the approach we recommend:
* You send your initial outreach campaign to your lead list from Klaviyo (with your custom message).
* In that campaign, you set the “Reply-To” address to an email that is connected to a Chatty inbox 
* When a lead replies to your email, their response will automatically appear inside Chatty as a new or updated conversation.
* From there, Chatty can take over the conversation
This setup allows you to keep using Klaviyo for scalable outbound email, while Chatty focuses on the inbound replies with your leads.

Just let me know if you need any further assistance - we're here to support you.

Best regards,
Megan - Chatty team.

What It Means
A <video> or <audio> element is present.
Why It Matters
<video> defines video, such as a movie clip or other video streams. <audio> defines sound, such as music or other audio streams. Audio-only content must have a transcript.
What To Do
For video content with audio, ensure that synchronized captioning and a transcript is provided. For audio-only content, ensure that a transcript is provided.

[CS: Jade] Hi there, 

This is Jade from the Avada Support Team, and I hope this note finds you well. 

In this email, I would like to follow up with you regarding your reported issues:

**1, Assign a unique FAQs block to each Collection page**

As we check, your theme has a single Default Collection template that includes many collections (https://capture.avada.io/i/skn2ELhGFls8), so it isn’t possible to assign a unique FAQs block to each collection within the same template. 

To achieve your goal of having a dedicated FAQs block per collection, the best approach would be to create separate Collection templates. You can then assign each template to a specific collection and add the corresponding FAQs block within that template. This way, each Collection will have its own independent FAQs without affecting others.

**2, The divider line appears thicker on some Collection pages**

This is related to the position of the FAQs blocks, as we can see. For example, on the Leggings collection, the FAQs block is placed closer to the product grid, so the separator appears thin (https://capture.avada.io/i/vT6l0qf8kukV). On other collections, such as Briques, the FAQs block is positioned further down, and the additional FAQs blocks create accumulated spacing or borders, making the line look thicker. (https://capture.avada.io/i/UNRziOi5MzaQ, https://capture.avada.io/i/cUqX91VBgZVO)

Once you separate the Collection template and add a unique FAQs block there, the divider will appear consistent across all pages.

Thank you for your patience and cooperation in this matter. If you have any other questions, please contact us for further support.

Regards,
Jade

[CS: Cody Hoang] @Cody Hoang, Away mode là tính năng cho từng staff member bật/tắt trạng thái "đang vắng" mà không cần logout khỏi app.

Away mode để làm gì:

• Khi staff bật Away → widget bên storefront sẽ coi staff đó là offline (dù vẫn đang login)
• Nếu tất cả staff đều Away → widget hiển thị như không có ai online → khách sẽ thấy trạng thái offline
• Dùng cho trường hợp staff cần nghỉ trưa, họp, hoặc tạm không muốn nhận chat mới mà không cần đăng xuất

Cách bật:

• Ở góc trên app Chatty (trong layout chính), có toggle "Away mode" — bật lên là xong

Lưu ý:

• Khi staff logout rồi login lại → Away mode tự động tắt (reset về false)
• Away mode chỉ ảnh hưởng trạng thái online của staff đó, không ảnh hưởng staff khác

Nói ngắn gọn: nó giống "Do Not Disturb" cho staff — vẫn login nhưng không nhận chat mới và hiện offline với khách 👍

这里有一个详细的回答，帮助你更好地理解离家模式的工作原理：

离家模式允许员工在不登出Chatty应用的情况下离线出现。
* 启用后，该员工不会在商店界面显示为可用。
* 如果所有员工都启用离家模式，小部件将向客户显示离线状态。

你可以在以下时间使用：适合休息、开会，或者任何想暂停进来聊天但保持登录状态时使用。

你还可以注意：这个设置只影响个人用户，不影响整个团队。

简而言之，离家模式就像“请勿打扰”——你保持登录状态，但不会被分配新的聊天窗口，也不会显示为在线。

希望这个回答能帮到你 <3

[CS: Phoebe] Step 1: Backup & Preparation: before starting, remember that chat history does not migrate from the app to the API.
Export Chats: Manually export important conversations.
Meta Business Manager: Have your Meta Business Manager ready.
Payment Method: Link a Credit/Debit card in Meta for conversation billing.

Step 2: Delete the Mobile WhatsApp Account: the number must be completely "free" before it can join the API.
Open the WhatsApp Business App on your phone.
Go to Settings > Account > Delete My Account.
Note: You must delete the account, not just uninstall the app.

Step 3: Create a WhatsApp Business Account (WABA)
Go to Meta Business Suite > Business Settings.
Navigate to Accounts > WhatsApp Accounts.
Click Add > Create a new WhatsApp Business Account.
Fill in your Business Profile (Display Name, Category, and Website).

Step 4: Verify Your Phone Number (OTP)
Enter your phone number into the Meta setup.
Choose SMS or Voice Call to receive the code.
Enter the 6-digit OTP code to verify ownership of the number.

Step 5: Enable Two-Step Verification (2FA): Meta requires this extra layer of security for API accounts.
In Business Settings, go to WhatsApp Accounts and select your account.
Click on Settings > WhatsApp Manager.
Go to Phone Numbers in the left menu.
Click the Settings (Gear icon) next to your number.

[CS: Cody] The AI first interprets the customer’s question to understand the intent, and then searches the available training data to generate the most relevant answer.

Because of that, it doesn’t always rely on exact keywords, but it performs best when the information in the training data is clearly defined and aligned with the types of questions customers are likely to ask.

In cases like the phone number, if it’s not strongly tied to certain intents (for example, general inquiries), the AI might not always include it unless the question is very clear.

If you’d like the AI to consistently provide contact details, we would recommend adding this information to the AI general instructions or setting up a scenario instruction (for example: whenever a customer asks about contacting the team, provide the email and phone number).

I can help add this and test the AI further to ensure it picks up the information more reliably. Would you like me to add this and test the AI for you?
[CS: Cody] I would recommend adding a scenario like this, as it helps the AI better recognize when to provide customers with contact details.

[CS: Technical] Hi there,



This is Ryan from the Avada Support Team. I hope you're doing well.



I wanted to follow up on the email deliverability issue you reported, where emails sent from Chatty Inbox are landing in spam despite the SPF record being updated.


After further investigation, we found that your current DMARC policy is set to p=quarantine. This means any emails that fail SPF or DKIM verification will automatically be routed to the spam/quarantine folder — which is likely the cause of the issue you're experiencing.



What we recommend:
Please update your DMARC DNS record to use p=none for now, so emails are no longer quarantined during testing:

v=DMARC1; p=none; adkim=r; aspf=r; rua=mailto:info@vopho.com;



Once updated, please allow a few minutes for DNS propagation, then send a test email from Chatty Inbox and check if it lands in the inbox correctly.



Please feel free to let us know the result after making the change, and we'll continue from there.



Should you have any further questions, don't hesitate to reach out.



Best regards,
Ryan
Avada Support Team

I’m following up regarding the issue where conversations handled by Maisa are not being counted in Analytics.

This is why the conversations handled by Maisa are not being counted in Analytics.

“Conversations will be assigned to whoever replies”

With this option enabled, conversations will be assigned to the team member who responds, and they will then be counted correctly in Analytics.

Since this behavior is based on the current setting (and not a system bug), we’re unfortunately unable to update the Analytics data for Maisa retroactively. We hope for your understanding in this case.

Please let me know if you need any help adjusting the settings - I’d be happy to assist.

Best regards,
Hazel
Chatty Support Team

[CS: Audrey] Hi there,

I hope you’re doing well. I wanted to let you know that the email delivery issue you encountered is due to an authentication problem with your domain (IOAKU.COM). Specifically, the message was rejected by Outlook because the domain’s DMARC policy failed, even though SPF and DKIM were detected.

To ensure your emails are successfully delivered and not blocked by recipients like Outlook, Gmail, or others, you will need to update your DMARC DNS record.

Please ask your domain or IT administrator to add or update the following DMARC record in your DNS settings:

_dmarc.ioaku.com    TXT    "v=DMARC1; p=none; rua=mailto:postmaster@ioaku.com"

DMARC helps verify that emails sent from your domain are legitimate. Without a valid DMARC policy, many email providers may reject or flag your messages as spam.

If you’re not sure how to update your DNS records, I recommend contacting your domain host (such as GoDaddy, Cloudflare, etc.) or your IT manager for further assistance.

Once the DMARC record is updated, delivery should start working normally again.

Avada team

Based on the synced data, the system only shows the sale price without the original price information ([screenshot](https://capture.avada.io/i/XdNe8j2tR0LM) || [screenshot](https://capture.avada.io/i/008iMitliEO2)). Could you please confirm if the original price on your product page is added manually or via a third-party app, rather than being stored in the standard Shopify "Compare at price" field?

**2. Measurements Conversion (Inches to Metric)**: 
After checking your current setup, we couldn't find a specific instruction file or guide for the AI on how to convert inches to the metric system. To prevent the AI from "hallucinating" and to ensure the most accurate calculations for your buyers, we highly recommend adding a clear conversion guide to the Knowledge Base.

**3. Shortening AI Answers**: 
We noticed that the instruction to "shorten answers" was duplicated in both your general settings and your custom knowledge file ([screenshot ](https://capture.avada.io/i/P3W07GoTFUAp)|| [screenshot](https://capture.avada.io/i/TjltbkbTtj4m)). We recommend avoiding duplicate info as it can confuse the AI. After temporarily removing the extra instructions, the AI's responses have become more concise. Please note that in some cases, the AI still needs to provide enough detail to ensure the buyer's questions are fully answered. We appreciate your understanding on this.

**4. Mentioning Bra Sizes (EU and US/UK)**: 
I have added specific instructions for the AI to mention sizes in both EU and US/UK formats ([screenshot](https://capture.avada.io/i/cPLJsxV2E8Du)). Could you please run a few tests on your end to see if the responses now meet your expectations?

**5. Booking Appointment Recommendations**: 
Regarding the AI recommending appointments, we found that your current instructions tell the AI to suggest a booking when it lacks sufficient size information ([screenshot](https://capture.avada.io/i/quMiJduagAQX)). In your test conversation, multiple different sizes were mentioned, which caused the AI to be uncertain about the exact size—hence the recommendation to book an appointment.

**6. Product Recommendations without Suitable Sizes**: 
After investigating the issue where the AI recommended the wrong products, we found a slight discrepancy in the data synchronization between Shopify and the AI Knowledge Base. I have performed a manual resync of your product data to ensure everything is up to date. I have also conducted several tests on my end, and the AI is now recommending the correct products as expected ([screenshot](https://capture.avada.io/i/raomPWoyKVvB)). Could you please check if the AI’s responses now match your expectations? I’d also appreciate it if you could run a few tests on your side to make sure everything is working smoothly. Site navigation
TUNO

FAQ
Frequently Asked Questions
Have a question? We’ve got you covered. Browse the answers below, or reach out to us directly at support@tunowear.com.

Orders & Shipping
Where do you ship to?
We currently ship to Nigeria, the United States, and Canada, with free standard shipping on all orders — no minimum required.

How long does delivery take?
Estimated delivery times after dispatch are:
• Nigeria: 15–30 days
• United States: 10–16 days
• Canada: 15–21 business days
Delivery times may vary due to customs clearance or public holidays.

How can I track my order?
Once your order ships, you’ll receive a confirmation email with a tracking number. Visit our Track Your Order page for real-time updates.

Can I change my shipping address after placing an order?
If your order hasn’t shipped yet, contact us immediately at support@tunowear.com and we’ll do our best to update it before dispatch.

Returns & Refunds
What is your return policy?
We accept returns within 10 days of delivery. Items must be unworn, unwashed, and in their original packaging with tags attached. See our full Refund Policy for details.

How do I initiate a return?
Email us at support@tunowear.com with your order number and reason for return. We’ll send you return instructions promptly.

Do you offer refunds or exchanges?
We offer store credit or exchanges for eligible returns. For items that arrive damaged, defective, or incorrect, we will provide a full refund, replacement, or resend at no cost to you.

What items are non-returnable?
Final sale items, hygiene products, custom or personalised pieces, and bundle products cannot be returned.

Sizing & Products
How do I find my size?
Visit our Size Guide for full measurement instructions and size charts across all product categories.

Do your products run true to size?
Yes, TUNO activewear is designed to fit true to size. If you’re between sizes, we recommend sizing up for comfort or sizing down for a compression fit.

What if my item arrives damaged or incorrect?
Contact us at support@tunowear.com with your order number and photos of the issue. We’ll resolve it quickly.

Payments & Security
What payment methods do you accept?
We accept major credit and debit cards, as well as other secure payment options available at checkout.

Is my payment information safe?
Absolutely. All transactions are processed through encrypted, secure payment gateways. Your data is never stored or shared.

Can I cancel my order?
Orders can be cancelled before they are dispatched. Once shipped, our return policy applies. Contact us as soon as possible at support@tunowear.com if you need to cancel.

Account & Support
Do I need an account to order?
No — you can check out as a guest. However, creating an account makes it easier to track orders and save your details for future purchases.

How do I reach customer support?
Email us at support@tunowear.com. Our team is available Monday–Friday and aims to respond within 24–48 hours.

Still Have Questions?
We’re always happy to help. Drop us a message at support@tunowear.com and we’ll get back to you as soon as possible.

[CS: Hazel] That’s a great question. This is actually very common when first setting up live chat. Here are a few tips to help increase the number of messages you receive:

1. Use proactive chat https://capture.avada.io/i/uUA9P9rHD1Yt
Set up a welcome or proactive message like:
* “Hi there! Need help choosing the right product?”
* “Get 10% off — just ask us here 👋”
This helps start conversations instead of waiting for customers to message first.

2. Offer an incentive: You can encourage engagement by offering something in return:
* Discount codes
* Free shipping
* First-time customer offers
For example:
“Chat with us to get a 10% discount code!”

3. Make the chat more visible
* Ensure the chat widget is clearly visible on all pages
* You can adjust position, timing, or animation to make it more noticeable

4. Use AI Assistant (24/7 replies)
Turn on AI so customers always get instant replies. This increases engagement since users are more likely to message when they know they’ll get a quick response.

5. Keep responses fast and helpful
The faster and more useful your replies are, the more customers will trust and use the chat again.

Thank you very much for your valuable feedback! 

Regarding your feedback about the AI's behavior when it cannot find an answer, we are currently redesigning the workflow so that the system will automatically hand over the conversation to a human agent, exactly as you described.

Please note that this is a significant update, and it will likely take **a few months** for us to officially release this feature. We appreciate your understanding and patience as we work to make this transition as smooth as possible.

In the meantime, we suggest two immediate solutions to help manage these situations:
1. Enhance the Knowledge Base: You can add the specific information the AI is currently missing to your training data (CSV or FAQ).
2. Add Custom Scenario Instructions: You can add a specific instruction to the AI to transfer the chat to a human if it feels it cannot assist further.
Here is a sample instruction you try on your Chatty scenario settings:
> *If you cannot find a definitive answer, or lack of data/knowledge/information in records, THEN you MUST stop attempting to answer and respond with the following EXACT phrase:*
> 
> *"I’ll forward this chat to the relevant team for review, and we’ll get back to you as soon as possible."*

1. Capture leads via Chatty
* Use chat, contact forms, or AI conversations to collect customer information (email/phone)
* Once a customer provides their details, Chatty can sync this data

2. Sync to Shopify as customers
* The collected information is automatically saved as a customer profile in Shopify
* This means your leads are already stored and ready for follow-up

3. Send data to marketing tools (optional but recommended)
* Integrate with tools like Klaviyo
* This allows you to segment and automate campaigns

4. Automate conversion flows
You can set up flows like:

* Welcome email after customer leaves their email
* Discount code for first purchase
* Abandoned cart reminders
* Follow-up messages after chat interaction

5. Convert to paying customers
With these automated flows, leads are nurtured and encouraged to complete a purchase

[CS: Hazel] To enable web browser notifications in Safari, please follow these steps:

On Mac (Safari):

* Open Safari
* Go to Safari → Settings (Preferences)
* Click the Websites tab
* Select Notifications
* Make sure “Allow websites to ask for permission to send notifications” is enabled
* Then visit the website again and click Allow when prompted

You can also manage notifications in: System Settings → Notifications → Safari / Website
[User] how about on iphone
[CS: Hazel] For Safari on iPhone, web push notifications are only supported on iOS 16.4 or later, and they require a few extra steps:

* Open Safari and visit the website
* Tap the Share icon (square with arrow) 
* Select “Add to Home Screen”
* Open the website again from the Home Screen (it will behave like an app)
* You should then see a prompt asking to Allow notifications → tap Allow

After that, you can manage notifications in: Settings → Notifications → (the website name)

Our tech team has reviewed the case and identified that the issue is related to this FAQ:
https://capture.avada.io/i/1RpWxRTFjthP

AI works based on context and similarity. When the customer mentioned that the price was high, the system detected an FAQ that includes content such as “if the price is high, check Pico Genie from £155,” and therefore automatically included that suggestion - even though the product in the conversation was not directly related.

To avoid this situation in the future, we recommend either:

* Revising the FAQ content to avoid general “alternative product suggestions” that may apply too broadly,
or
* Adjusting the Topic to make it more specific (for example, applying it only to the Pico Genie category).

This will help the AI provide more accurate and context-relevant responses. Please let us know if you’d like assistance in adjusting the FAQ settings - we’re happy to help.

Best regards,
